response.justification: This candidate has exceptional technical depth with 19+ years of software development and MIT credentials, but lacks the required 2 years of people management experience. While holding multiple staff-level titles, the resume details only individual contributor work without evidence of team leadership, performance management, hiring, or direct reports. For a full-time people-leadership role, the absence of management experience is a disqualifying gap that technical excellence cannot overcome.
strengths: Exceptional technical foundation with 19+ years across multiple stacks and proven expertise in developer tools (C IDE at National Instruments), testing frameworks, CI/CD infrastructure, and software craftsmanship practices
Strong mentorship and knowledge-sharing background demonstrated through workshops (Web Internals), structured training courses (Selenium), and initiatives (Refactoring Dojos, Code Kata sessions)
Entrepreneurial and independent leadership shown through founding GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed in production and used by conservation organizations
concerns: Complete absence of people management experience—no mention of direct reports, performance reviews, one-on-ones, hiring decisions, or formal team development responsibilities, which are core job requirements

Technical leadership scope unclear despite staff titles—resume shows individual contributions rather than evidence of influencing roadmaps, guiding system designs at team level, or mentoring engineers through formal structures

Developer tools background borderline—1.5 years at National Instruments IDE work, partial infrastructure experience at Altom, and very recent MCP tooling work at re:cinq (1 month) may not clearly meet 3-year requirement

key_factors.factor: Extensive technical expertise and software craftsmanship
